I found a dedicated `apps/prototype-org-next` Vite app. I’m checking its local config and dev orchestration so every port reference moves together.

**Troubleshooting package issues**

It seems I can't find "rg broad," which makes me think there might be an issue with the package.json script. I should take a look at the scripts in the package or vite, particularly the dev section. Let's explore that further to see if I can identify what's going wrong there. I want to make sure I get this right so I can help sort out the issue!

**Updating package version**

I'm working on editing the package and updating Vite from version 3209 to 3212. I think I'll use ApplyPatch for this update. I wonder if I actually need to check the documentation for it? Maybe I can skip that part. It might also be a good idea to search the entire repository for references to version 3209 just to make sure everything is in sync. Let's see how it goes!
Only two explicit `3209` references are in the prototype app itself, so I’m updating the Vite dev script and server config to `3212`.

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/prototype-org-next/package.json
@@
   "type": "module",
   "scripts": {
-    "dev": "vite --port 3209",
+    "dev": "vite --port 3212",
     "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
     "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
     "clean": "rm -rf .turbo && rm -rf node_modules dist"
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch

content.text:

`apps/prototype-org-next/vite.config.ts`

Verified there are no remaining `3209` references. I didn’t run the dev server.

`ui-next-catalog` is currently on `3210`, which conflicts with `org-next`. I’m reverting the prototype app back to `3209` and changing the catalog app from `3210` to `3212`.
